article_title: Crimson Desert Launches March 19 as Pearl Abyss Bets Its Proprietary Engine Against Unreal on a 150 GB Open World
summary: Multiple source misattribution issues found. Claims attributed to PC Gamer and Kotaku are not present in those sources.

findings.message: Source misattribution: claims attributed to PC Gamer not found in source
findings.details: The article attributes the following to PC Gamer but they do not appear in the cited article: (1) 'directional inputs alter grapple animations', (2) 'three skill specializations: archery for ranged combat, tanking built around parries, and stealth', (3) 'spears' listed as a weapon type. PC Gamer mentions swords, shields, axes, and bows but not spears.

findings.message: Source misattribution: BlackSpace Engine claim attributed to Kotaku not found in source
findings.details: The article states 'a former developer described as exceeding typical Unreal Engine 5 productions in technical ambition, according to early preview coverage reported by Kotaku' but the Kotaku article is a weekly game release roundup that contains no such technical analysis, no developer quotes, and no mention of the BlackSpace Engine or Unreal Engine 5.

Multiple specific claims lack sourcing from any of the three cited articles: $69.99 price, $79.99 Deluxe, $279.99 Collector's Edition, 150 GB install size, pre-loads on March 17, embargo March 18 at 3 PM PT, 50+ hours of content in a single region. These may be accurate but are not supported by the cited sources.

findings.message: macOS platform claim not confirmed by cited sources
findings.details: The article claims macOS as a launch platform. PlayStation Blog only covers PS5; PC Gamer does not mention Mac; Kotaku lists PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC but does not mention macOS.

content_preview.title: Crimson Desert Launches March 19 as Pearl Abyss Bets Its Proprietary Engine Against Unreal on a 150 GB Open World
content_preview.summary: Pearl Abyss ships its open-world action game Crimson Desert on PS5, Xbox, PC, and Mac, with leaked impressions praising combat but flagging inventory issues.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Pearl Abyss is set to release Crimson Desert on March 19, 2026, delivering a single-player open-world action game across PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, PC, and macOS simultaneously. The title, built on the South Korean studio's proprietary BlackSpace Engine rather than the industry-standard Unreal Engine 5, asks $69.99 for a standard edition and ships with a 150 GB install footprint on PC. A review embargo lifts on March 18 at 3 PM PT, one day before launch, while pre-loads went li...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.source_verification: All three source URLs are accessible. PlayStation Blog (Source 1): Confirmed release date March 19, PS5 coverage, traversal mechanics, combat basics, PS5 Pro PSSR/ray tracing/DualSense features. PC Gamer (Source 2): Confirmed melee combat, wrestling moves, throwing enemies. However, claims attributed to PC Gamer that are NOT present: 'directional inputs alter grapple animations', 'three skill specializations (archery, tanking, stealth)', and 'spears' as weapon. PC Gamer mentions bows, not spears. Kotaku (Source 3): Weekly game release roundup listing Crimson Desert for March 19 on PS5/Xbox/PC. No mention of BlackSpace Engine, UE5, or any developer quotes about technical ambition.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Multiple unsourced claims: $69.99/$79.99/$279.99 pricing tiers, 150 GB install size, embargo March 18 at 3 PM PT, pre-loads March 17, 50+ hours of content. macOS as a launch platform not confirmed by any cited source. These may be accurate but need proper sourcing.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and informative tone. No editorializing or sensationalism. Appropriate for News category.
concerns: BlackSpace Engine vs UE5 claim falsely attributed to Kotaku — Kotaku article contains no such content
Directional grapple inputs and skill specializations falsely attributed to PC Gamer
Spears listed as weapon type but PC Gamer mentions bows, not spears
Numerous specific factual claims (pricing, install size, embargo, pre-loads, hours of content) not supported by any cited source
macOS platform claim unsupported by cited sources
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REQUEST_CHANGES. The article demonstrates solid structure and neutral tone but suffers from multiple source misattribution errors where claims are presented as coming from specific sources that do not contain them. This is a fundamental editorial integrity issue that must be corrected before publication.
